ORDER GRANTING, IN PART, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES BY DEFENDANTS SHEA TECHNOLOGY AND LAWRENCE E. SHEA AND AWARDING MONETARY SANCTIONS

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: CURT HOLBREICH, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, HOWARD RICE NEMEROVSKI CANADY FALK & RABKIN. CONSTANTIN VINCENT ROBOOSTOFF, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS BY DEFENDANTS SHEA TECHNOLOGY AND LAWRENCE SHEA IS GRANTED IN PART AND MOOT IN PART. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER AS TO THOSE RESPONSES. AS DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SUPPLEMENT CERTAIN INTERROGATORIES SET FORTH IN THE OPPOSITION, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AND ORDERS PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND FURTHER AS THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE HOLBREICH DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INADEQUATE AND THE LATE RESPONSES AND LATE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $763.80, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
